concept.headline: Crystal uses if/unless and case/when for clear branching.
concept.explanation: Branching lets your program choose actions based on conditions. Crystal keeps syntax readable and explicit.
concept.syntax:
score = 82
grade = if score >= 90
  "A"
elsif score >= 80
  "B"
else
  "C"
end
puts grade
keyPoints: Readable control flow prevents logic errors

demo.intro: Trace how Crystal decides a grade from score.
demo.code:
score = 82
grade = if score >= 90
  "A"
elsif score >= 80
  "B"
else
  "C"
end
puts grade

explanation.misconception: Once true branch matches, others are skipped
Output reflects final selected branch only

prediction.question: If score = 70 in the same code, what prints?
prediction.explanation: 70 fails >= 90 and >= 80, so else branch prints C.

explanation.afterDemo: Clear branch ordering is essential. Start specific checks first, then broader fallbacks.
keyTakeaways: Branch checks run top to bottom.

practice.prompt: Set temp = 31. Print "hot" if temp >= 30, else print "ok".
practice.solutionCode:
temp = 31
if temp >= 30
  puts "hot"
else
  puts "ok"
end

feedback.successMessage: Correct. Your branch selected the expected output.

summary.title: Control Flow — Complete
takeaways: Crystal branching is explicit and readable.
